items.back: Monitor interphones and assigned radio frequencies continuously.
As necessary, authorize a pilot to interrupt communications guard for a safety-related problem requiring company communication.
Terminate interphone messages with your operating initials.
If needed for clarity, restate numbers using either group or single-digit form.
Use the full identification in reply.

items.front: U.S. registry civil aircraft initial contact prefix
items.back: November, followed by ICAO phonetic pronunciation of the registration numbers/letters.
Call sign followed by the flight number in group form; single digits may be used for clarity.

items.front: Air taxi/commercial operator without FAA-authorized call sign
items.back: Use TANGO on initial contact if used by the pilot, followed by registration number.

items.front: Traffic information aircraft type description: super/heavy exception
items.back: Except for super and heavy aircraft, describe aircraft by military, air carrier, civil, or other type conventions in 2-4-21.
